title: Arrays, Slices, Backing Storage, Append, Copy, and Capacity · Execution Trace: Append Copy Aliasing
summary: A batch processor must window records, grow buffers, preserve caller data, and avoid retention or aliasing defects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go append copy aliasing increment in an alias-safe batch and buffer toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict whether append reuses or replaces storage and use copy or clone patterns to establish ownership.
Use two-index and full slice expressions while proving bounds, capacity, and append authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Append Copy Aliasing · Theory Append Copy Aliasing
steps.instruction:
theory case — A batch slice exposes 5 of 9 records from shared storage. Append one result, mutate one element, clone one boundary, and predict length, capacity, aliasing, and retained storage after every operation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Append Copy Aliasing contract for the supplied data.
